title: heliotropium convolvulaceum var. racemosum
intro: Heliotropium convolvulaceum var. racemosum is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant, commonly referred to as a type of heliotrope or turnsole, that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Its health benefits include treating conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as providing relief from skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, Heliotropium convolvulaceum var. racemosum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed internally to harness its medicinal properties.
